text.en: Flip a coin. If heads, the Defending Pokémon is now Confused.

flavor_text.en: Its brain can outperform a supercomputer. Its intelligence quotient is said to be 5000.

text.en: Does 10 damage plus 10 more damage for each Energy card attached to the Defending Pokémon.
Discard 1 Energy card attached to Mewtwo in order to use this attack. During your opponent's next turn, prevent all effects of attacks, including damage, done to Mewtwo.

flavor_text.en: A scientist created this Pokémon after years of horrific gene-splicing and DNA engineering experiments.

text.en: Flip a coin. If heads, Defending Pokémon is now Poisoned.

flavor_text.en: Often found in forests, eating leaves. It has a sharp, venomous stinger on its head.

rules_text.en: Discard 2 of the other cards from your hand in order to search your deck for any card and put it into your hand. Shuffle your deck afterward.
Your opponent shuffles his or her hand into his or her deck, then draws 7 cards.
Discard 2 of the other cards from your hand in order to put a Trainer card from your discard pile into your hand.
You and your opponent show each other your hands, then shuffle all the Trainer cards from your hands into your decks.
Put a Stage 2 Evolution card from your hand on the matching Basic Pokémon. You can only play this card when you would be allowed to evolve that Pokémon anyway.
Trade 1 of the Basic Pokémon or Evolution cards in your hand for 1 of the Basic Pokémon or Evolution cards from your deck. Show both cards to your opponent. Shuffle your deck afterward.
Choose 1 of your Pokémon in play and return its Basic Pokémon card to your hand. (Discard all cards attached to that card.)
Discard 1 Energy card attached to 1 of your own Pokémon in order to choose 1 of your opponent's Pokémon and up to 2 Energy cards attached to it. Discard those Energy cards.
Attach Defender to 1 of your Pokémon. At the end of your opponent's next turn, discard Defender. Damage done to that Pokémon by attacks is reduced by 20 (after applying Weakness and Resistance).

text.en: Flip a coin. If heads, this attack does 30 damage plus 10 more damage; if tails, this attack does 30 damage and Nidoking does 10 damage to itself.
The Defending Pokémon is now Poisoned. It now takes 20 Poison damage instead of 10 after each player's turn (even if it was already Poisoned).

flavor_text.en: Uses its powerful tail in battle to smash, constrict, then break its prey's bones.

rules_text.en: Trade 1 of the other cards in your hand for up to 2 basic Energy cards from your discard pile.
Your Active Pokémon is no longer Asleep, Confused, Paralyzed, or Poisoned.
Shuffle 2 of the other cards from your hand into your deck in order to draw a card.

Attach PlusPower to your Active Pokémon. At the end of your turn, discard PlusPower. If this Pokémon's attack does damage to the Defending Pokémon (after applying Weakness and Resistance), the attack does 10 more damage to the Defending Pokémon.
Remove all damage counters from all of your own Pokémon with damage counters on them, then discard all Energy cards attached to those Pokémon.
Look at up to 5 cards from the top of your deck and rearrange them as you like.
Discard your hand, then draw 7 cards.
Discard 1 Energy card attached to 1 of your own Pokémon in order to remove up to 4 damage counters from that Pokémon.
Draw 2 cards.
Choose 1 Energy card attached to 1 of your opponent's Pokémon and discard it.

text.en: Does 20 damage plus 20 more damage for each Nidoking you have in play.

flavor_text.en: Its hard scales provide strong protection. It uses its hefty bulk to execute powerful moves.

rules_text.en: Choose 1 of your opponent's Benched Pokémon and switch it with his or her Active Pokémon.
Flip a coin. If heads, you may search your deck for any Basic Pokémon or Evolution card. Show that card to your opponent, then put it into your hand. Shuffle your deck afterward.
Remove up to 2 damage counters from 1 of your Pokémon.
Switch 1 of your Benched Pokémon with your Active Pokémon.
Provides {C}{C} energy. Doesn't count as a basic Energy card.

text.en: If your opponent has any Benched Pokémon, choose 1 of them and switch it with the Defending Pokémon.
Discard 1 Energy card attached to Ninetales in order to use this attack.

flavor_text.en: Very smart and very vengeful. Grabbing one of its many tails could result in a 1,000-year curse.

text.en: Unless this attack Knocks Out the Defending Pokémon, return the Defending Pokémon and all cards attached to it to your opponent's hand.

flavor_text.en: When hunting, it skims the surface of water at high speed to pick off unwary prey such as Magikarp.

text.en: Does 30 damage plus 10 more damage for each Water Energy attached to Poliwrath but not used to pay for this attack's Energy cost. Extra Energy after the 2nd doesn't count.
If the Defending Pokémon has any Energy cards attached to it, choose 1 and discard it.

flavor_text.en: An adept swimmer at both the front crawl and breaststroke. Easily overtakes the best human swimmers.

text.en: Flip a coin. If heads, during your opponent's next turn, prevent all effects of attacks, including damage, done to Raichu.
Flip a coin. If tails, Raichu does 30 damage to itself.

flavor_text.en: Its long tail serves as a ground to protect itself from its own high-voltage power.

text.en: During your next turn, Scyther's Slash attack's base damage is 60 instead of 30.

flavor_text.en: With ninja-like agility and speed, it can create the illusion that there is more than one of it.
This plant blooms when it is absorbing solar energy. It stays on the move to seek sunlight.

text.en: The Defending Pokémon is now Asleep.
Does 10 damage plus 10 more damage for each of your Benched Pokémon.

flavor_text.en: The body is soft and rubbery. When angered, it will suck in air and inflate itself to an enormous size.

text.en: Does 40 damage plus 10 more damage for each Water Energy attached to Blastoise but not used to pay for this attack's Energy cost. You can't add more than 20 damage in this way.

flavor_text.en: A brutal Pokémon with pressurized water jets on its shell. They are used for high-speed tackles.

text.en: Flip a coin. If tails, Zapdos does 30 damage to itself.
Discard all Energy cards attached to Zapdos in order to use this attack.

flavor_text.en: A legendary bird Pokémon said to appear from clouds while wielding enormous lightning bolts.

text.en: Flip 2 coins. This attack does 30 damage times the number of heads.
Flip a coin. If heads, the Defending Pokémon is now Poisoned.

flavor_text.en: Flies at high speed and attacks using the large, venomous stingers on its forelegs and tail.

text.en: Flip 2 coins. This attack does 30 damage times the number of heads.
If the Defending Pokémon has any Energy cards attached to it, choose 1 of them and discard it.

flavor_text.en: A mystical Pokémon that exudes a gentle aura. Has the ability to change climate conditions.

text.en: Does 10 damage to each of your own Benched Pokémon. (Don't apply Weakness and Resistance for Benched Pokémon.)

flavor_text.en: A team of Diglett triplets. It triggers huge earthquakes by burrowing 60 miles underground.

text.en: Flip a coin. If heads, the Defending Pokémon is now Paralyzed.
Flip a coin. If heads, this attack does 30 damage plus 10 more damage; if tails, this attack does 30 damage and Electabuzz does 10 damage to itself.

flavor_text.en: Normally found near power plants, it can wander away and cause major blackouts in cities.

text.en: Flip a coin. If tails, Electrode does 10 damage to itself.

flavor_text.en: It stores electrical energy under very high pressure. It often explodes with little or no provocation.

text.en: Draw a card.
Flip 4 coins. This attack does 20 damage times the number of heads.

flavor_text.en: The infant rarely ventures out of its mother's protective pouch until it is three years old.

text.en: Does 10 damage plus 10 more damage for each damage counter on the Defending Pokémon.

flavor_text.en: If interrupted while miming, it will slap around the enemy with its broad hands.

text.en: If your opponent has any Benched Pokémon, he or she chooses 1 of them and switches it with the Defending Pokémon. (Do the damage before switching the Pokémon.)
If Pidgeotto was attacked last turn, do the final result of that attack on Pidgeotto to the Defending Pokémon.

flavor_text.en: Very protective of its sprawling territory, this Pokémon will fiercely peck at any intruder.

text.en: Flip a coin. If heads, the Defending Pokémon is now Paralyzed.

flavor_text.en: If it fails to crush the victim in its pincers, it will swing its victim around and toss it hard.

text.en: Flip a coin. If heads, prevent all damage done to Chansey during your opponent's next turn. (Any other effects of attacks still happen.)
Chansey does 80 damage to itself.

flavor_text.en: A rare and elusive Pokémon that is said to bring happiness to those who manage to catch it.

text.en: Flip a coin. If heads, the Defending Pokémon is now Paralyzed.

flavor_text.en: Very lazy. Just eats and sleeps. As its rotund bulk builds, it becomes steadily more slothful.

text.en: Flip a coin. If heads, the Defending Pokémon is now Confused and Poisoned.

flavor_text.en: The dust-like scales covering its wings are color coded to indicate the kinds of poison it has.

text.en: If your opponent has any Benched Pokémon, choose 1 of them and switch it with his or her Active Pokémon.
Flip a coin. If heads, the Defending Pokémon can't retreat during your opponent's next turn.

flavor_text.en: Said to live in huge colonies deep in jungles, although no one has ever returned from there.

text.en: Discard 1 Energy card attached to Arcanine in order to use this attack.
Arcanine does 30 damage to itself.

flavor_text.en: A Pokémon that has been long admired for its beauty. It runs gracefully, as if on wings.

text.en: If your opponent has any Benched Pokémon, he or she chooses 1 of them and switches it with the Defending Pokémon. (Do the damage before switching the Pokémon.)
Remove a number of damage counters from Butterfree equal to half the damage done to the Defending Pokémon (after applying Weakness and Resistance) (rounded up to the nearest 10).

flavor_text.en: In battle, it flaps its wings at high speed to release highly toxic dust into the air.

text.en: Discard 1 Energy card attached to Charmeleon in order to use this attack.

flavor_text.en: When it swings its burning tail, it raises the temperature to unbearably high levels.

text.en: Flip a coin. If heads, the Defending Pokémon is now Paralyzed.

flavor_text.en: Stores thermal energy in its body. Swims at a steady 8 knots even in intensely cold waters.

text.en: Does 10 damage plus 10 more damage for each damage counter on Dodrio.

flavor_text.en: Uses its three brains to execute complex plans. While two heads sleep, one head stays awake.
Long considered a mythical Pokémon until recently, when a small colony was found living underwater.

text.en: Switch Exeggutor with 1 of your Benched Pokémon.
Flip a number of coins equal to the number of Energy attached to Exeggutor. This attack does 20 damage times the number of heads.

flavor_text.en: Legend has it that on rare occasions, one of its heads will drop off and continue on as an Exeggcute.

text.en: Discard 2 Energy cards attached to Charizard in order to use this attack.

flavor_text.en: Spits fire that is hot enough to melt boulders. Known to unintentionally cause forest fires.

text.en: Flip a coin. If tails, this attack does nothing. Either way, you can't use this attack again as long as Farfetch'd stays in play (even putting Farfetch'd on the Bench won't let you use it again.)

flavor_text.en: The sprig of green onions it holds is its weapon. This sprig is used much like a metal sword.

text.en: Flip a coin. If heads, during your opponent's next turn, prevent all effects of attacks, including damage, done to Fearow.

flavor_text.en: With its huge and magnificent wings, it can keep aloft without ever having to land for rest.
Very protective of its territory. It will bark and bite to repel intruders from its space.

text.en: The Defending Pokémon is now Asleep.
You can't this attack unless the Defending Pokémon is Asleep.

flavor_text.en: Because of its ability to slip through block walls, it is said to be from another dimension.

text.en: The Defending Pokémon is now Poisoned.

flavor_text.en: When the bulb on its back grows large, the Pokémon seems to lose the ability to stand on its hind legs.

text.en: Flip 2 coins. This attack does 10 damage times the number of heads.
Does 20 damage plus 10 more damage for each damage counter on the Defending Pokémon.

flavor_text.en: Merely by meditating, the Pokémon launches a powerful psychic energy attack.

text.en: Discard 1 Energy card attached to Kadabra in order use this attack. Remove all damage counters from Kadabra.

flavor_text.en: It emits special alpha waves from its body that induce headaches even to those just nearby.

text.en: Flip a coin. If heads, prevent all damage done to Kakuna during your opponent's next turn. (Any other effects of attacks still happen.)
Flip a coin. If heads, the Defending Pokémon is now Poisoned.

flavor_text.en: Almost incapable of moving, this Pokémon can only harden its shell to protect itself from predators.

text.en: Flip a coin. If heads, the Defending Pokémon is now Paralyzed.
Flip a coin. If heads, the Defending Pokémon is now Confused.

flavor_text.en: Its tongue can be extended like a chameleon's. It leaves a stinging sensation when it licks enemies.

text.en: Does 50 damage minus 10 for each damage counter on Machoke.
Machoke does 20 damage to itself.

flavor_text.en: Its muscular body is so powerful that it must wear a power-save belt to help regulate its motions.

text.en: Choose 1 of the Defending Pokémon's attacks. Metronome copies that attack except for its Energy costs and anything else required in order to use that attack, such as discarding Energy cards. (No matter what type the Defending Pokémon is, Clefable's type is still Colorless.)
All damage done by attacks to Clefable during your opponent's next turn is reduce by 20 (after applying Weakness and Resistance).

flavor_text.en: A timid Fairy Pokémon that is rarely seen. It will run and hide the moment it senses people.

text.en: Does 10 damage times number of damage counters on Magikarp.

flavor_text.en: In the distant past, it was stronger than its horribly weak descendants that exist today.

text.en: Discard 1 Energy card attached to Magmar in order to use this attack.

flavor_text.en: Its body always burns with an orange glow that enables it to hide perfectly among flames.

flavor_text.en: The bone it holds is its key weapon. It throws the bone skillfully like a boomerang to K.O. targets.

text.en: Flip a coin. If heads, the Defending Pokémon is now Confused.

flavor_text.en: The female's horn develops slowly. Prefers physical attacks such as clawing and biting.

text.en: Flip 2 coins. This attack does 30 damage times the number of heads.

flavor_text.en: An aggressive Pokémon that is quick to attack. The horn on its head secretes a powerful venom.

text.en: The Defending Pokémon is now Asleep.

flavor_text.en: A host-parasite pair in which the parasite mushroom has taken over the host bug. Prefers damp places.

text.en: If the Defending Pokémon attacks Persian during your opponent's next turn, any damage done by the attack is reduce by 10 (after applying Weakness and Resistance). (Benching either Pokémon ends this effect.)

flavor_text.en: Although its fur has many admirers, it is tough to raise as a pet because of its fickle meanness.

text.en: Choose 1 of defenders attacks. Defender cannot use that attack next turn.
Flip 2 coins. This attack does 30 damage times number of heads.

flavor_text.en: Capable of living in or out of water. When out of water, it sweats to keep its body slimy.

text.en: Does damage to the Defending Pokémon equal to half the Defending Pokémon's remaining HP (rounded up to the nearest 10).

flavor_text.en: It uses its whiskers to maintain its balance. It seems to slow down if they are cut off.

text.en: Rhydon does 20 damage to itself. If your opponent has any Benched Pokémon, he or she chooses 1 of them and switches it with the Defending Pokémon. (Do the damage before switching the Pokémon. Switch the Pokémon even if Rhydon is knocked out.)

flavor_text.en: Protected by an armor-like hide, it is capable of living in molten lava of 3600 degrees.

text.en: Flip a coin. If heads, the Defending Pokémon is now Asleep.

Choose 1 of Defending Pokémon's attacks. Metronome copies that attack except for its Energy costs and anything else required in order to use that attack, such as discarding energy cards. (No matter what type the defender is, Clefairy's type is still Colorless.)

flavor_text.en: Its magical and cute appeal has many admirers. It is rare and found only in certain areas.
In the autumn spawning season, they can be seen swimming powerfully up rivers and creeks.
The protruding horn on its head is very hard. This horn is used for bashing through thick ice.

text.en: Flip a coin. If heads, this attack does 20 damage plus 10 more damage; if tails, this attack does 20 damage.
Does 20 damage plus 10 more damage for each damage counter on Tauros. Flip a coin. If tails, Tauros is now Confused (after doing damage).

flavor_text.en: When it targets an enemy, it charges furiously while whipping its body with its long tails.

flavor_text.en: Often hides in water to stalk unweary prey. When swimming quickly, it moves its ears to maintain balance.

text.en: Flip a coin. If heads, the Defending Pokémon is now Poisoned.

flavor_text.en: It spits out poisonpowder to immobilize the enemy, and then finishes the enemy with a spray of acid.

text.en: Flip a coin. If heads, the Defending Pokémon is now Paralyzed.

flavor_text.en: Using its ability to read minds, it will identify impending danger and teleport to safety.

text.en: Search your deck for a Basic Pokémon named Bellsprout and put it onto your Bench. Shuffle your deck afterward. (You can't use this attack if your Bench is full.)

flavor_text.en: A carnivorous Pokémon that traps and eats bugs. It uses its root feet to soak up needed moisture.

text.en: Unless all damage from this attack is prevented, you may remove 1 damage counter from Bulbasaur.

flavor_text.en: A strange seed was planted on its back at birth. Thus, a plant sprouted and now grows with this Pokémon.

text.en: Flip a coin. If heads, the Defending Pokémon is now Paralyzed.

flavor_text.en: Its short feet are tipped with suction pads that enable it to tirelessly climb slopes and walls.

text.en: Discard 1 Energy card attached to Charmander in order to use this attack.

flavor_text.en: Obviously prefers hot places. If it gets caught in the rain, steam is said to spout from the tip of its tail.

text.en: Flip a coin. If heads, Defending Pokémon is now Paralyzed.

flavor_text.en: Rarely seen in the wild. Huge and vicious, it is capable of destroying entire cities in a rage.

text.en: If the Defending Pokémon attacks Cubone during your opponent's next turn, any damage done by the attack is reduced by 20 (after applying Weakness and Resistance). (Benching either Pokémon ends this effect.)
Does 10 damage plus 10 more damage for each damage counter on Cubone.

flavor_text.en: Because it never removes its skull helmet, no one has ever seen this Pokémon's real face.
Lives about three feet underground, where it feeds on plant roots. It sometimes appears above ground.

text.en: Flip 2 coins. This attack does 10 damage times the number of heads.

flavor_text.en: A bird that makes up for its poor flying with its fast foot speed. Leaves giant footprints.

flavor_text.en: Puts enemies to sleep, then eats their dreams. Occasionally gets sick from eating bad dreams.

flavor_text.en: Often mistaken for eggs. When disturbed, they quickly gather and attack in swarms.

flavor_text.en: Almost invisible, this gaseous Pokémon cloaks the target and puts it to sleep without notice.
Its tail fin billows like an elegant ballroom dress, giving it the nickname "Water Queen."

text.en: The Defending Pokémon is now Asleep.

flavor_text.en: When its huge eyes light up, it sings a mysteriously soothing melody that lulls its enemies to sleep.
Loves to build its muscles. It trains in all styles of martial arts to become even stronger.

text.en: Flip a coin. If heads, the Defending Pokémon is now Paralyzed.
Does 10 damage to each Pokémon on each player's Bench. (Don't apply Weakness and Resistance for Benched Pokémon.) Magnemite does 40 damage to itself.

flavor_text.en: Uses anti-gravity to stay suspended. Appears without warning and uses attacks like Thunder Wave.
While seeming to do nothing, it fires punches in lightning-fast volleys that are impossible to see.

text.en: Flip a coin. If heads, draw a card.

flavor_text.en: Adores circular objects. Wanders the streets on a nightly basis to look for dropped loose change.

Flip a coin. If heads, the Defending Pokémon is now Paralyzed.

flavor_text.en: It is vulnerable to attack because its shell is soft, exposing its weak and tender body.

flavor_text.en: Although small, its venomous barbs make this Pokémon dangerous. The female has smaller horns.

flavor_text.en: Stiffens its ears to sense danger. The larger, more powerful of its horns secretes venom.

flavor_text.en: As it grows, the stone portions of its body harden to become similar to a diamond, though colored black.

flavor_text.en: Burrows to suck tree roots. The mushrooms on its back grow by drawing nutrients from the bug host.

flavor_text.en: A common sight in forests and woods. It flaps its wings at ground level to kick up blinding sand.

flavor_text.en: When several of these Pokémon gather, their electricity can cause lightning storms.

flavor_text.en: Its newly grown legs prevent it from running. It appears to prefer swimming over trying to stand.

flavor_text.en: Its massive bones are 1,000 times harder than human bones. It can easily knock a trailer flying.

text.en: If the Defending Pokémon tries to attack during your opponent's next turn, your opponent flips a coin. If tails, that attack does nothing.

flavor_text.en: Burrows deep underground in arid locations far from water. It only emerges to hunt for food.

flavor_text.en: Eats bugs in grassy areas. It has to flap its short wings at high speeds to stay airborne.

flavor_text.en: Its central core glows with the seven colors of the rainbow. Some people value this core as a gem.

flavor_text.en: Its whole body is swathed with wide vines that are similar to seaweed. These vines shake as it walks.

flavor_text.en: Lives in the shadows of tall trees where it eats insects. It is attracted by light at night.
Usually found in power plants. Easily mistaken for a Poké Ball, it has zapped many people.

text.en: Flip a coin. If heads, the Defending Pokémon is now Confused.

flavor_text.en: At the time of birth, it has just one tail. Its tail splits from the tip as it grows older.
